name: Reaction
summary: Affix-a-symbol-to-content interaction: each reaction is an `aria-pressed` toggle backed by the Reaction Intent. Composes the expressive-symbol glyph (never re-renders it) and adopts Atlassian ReactionSummary as the contract baseline (count · users[] · reacted · optimisticallyUpdated). The only first-class precedent (Atlassian @atlaskit/reactions) is exactly this block+intent shape. Per #370 Fork 5.
designDecisions: Block + intent (#370 Fork 5): reaction IS runnable behavior (toggle-your-own, aggregate, optimistic update), so the standard owns the mechanics once instead of leaving every consumer to re-implement them — an intent alone would under-specify the very thing that makes a reaction a reaction.

Contract baseline = Atlassian `ReactionSummary`: `count` (aggregate) · `users[]` (who-reacted roster, display-limited) · `reacted` (toggle-your-own) · `optimisticallyUpdated`. Classified a DATA CONTRACT, NOT a protocol — no swappable-vendor/engine-interop story, so a protocol would be lock-in for no interop gain.

Multi-user sync is COMPOSED, never baked in: the "others see your reaction" concern is a separate real-time protocol (webrealtime); the reaction contract stays sync-agnostic. Count changes announce through `live-region-status`.

Accessibility (load-bearing): each reaction is an `aria-pressed` toggle with a COMPOSED accessible name (emoji name + count + your-reacted-state — "thumbs up, 5 reactions, you reacted, activate to remove"), not a bare glyph. Announce YOUR LOCAL toggle only — never every remote tick. The roster is keyboard-reachable.

Reaction set is an open, config-driven named-set registry (#370 Fork 6): default-less core, convergent `sentiment` default, `allowAllEmojis` escape to the full picker. Composes the expressive-symbol render strategy for the glyphs.
vs #354 status badge: a reaction count chip resembles a `surface`-tokened badge but means "N people reacted," not "this state" — they compose, they do not merge.

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: Each reaction is an `aria-pressed` toggle button affixing a symbol to content; Space/Enter toggle the pressed state.
